summary.scholar: On April 9, 1865, Confederate General Robert E. Lee surrendered to Union General Ulysses S. Grant at Appomattox Court House, Virginia, effectively ending the Civil War.
summary.explorer: On April 9, 1865, General Lee surrendered to General Grant. After four long years, the Civil War was finally over.

significance.scholar: The surrender preserved the United States as one nation.
significance.explorer: This meant the Southern states would rejoin the United States. The country was going to stay together.
